titleStmt.title: Letter from Lord French to Sir John Maxwell, 1 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Denton Pinkstone French

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Lord French, John Denton Pinkstone French, 1st Earl of Ypres (1852-1925), commander of all British forces on home service, 1916-1918, to Sir John Maxwell (1859-1929), primarily responsible for government policy in the immediate aftermath of the Rising. The letter was written in the aftermath of the Easter Rising in Dublin, before the first executions of the Rising's leaders. Lord French congratulates Maxwell on the 'good work which has been done in Ireland'. He says he showed Maxwell's letter to the Prime Minister who was 'very pleased with it all'. French indicates that he has made enquiries about getting ten Camalters (shire horses) for Maxwell's cavalry.

correspAction.placeName: G.H.Q. Home Forces, Horse Guards, Whitehall, S.W., London

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
G.H.Q. Home Forces,
Horse Guards,
Whitehall, S.W. May. 1. 1916 My Dear Maxwell is
my luck to you and
is taking these
few lines. I congratulate you on
the good work which
has been done in Ireland. I was with the
Prime Minister this
afternoon & I showed
him your letter &
report. He is very
worried with it
all.
I asked you again,
what the Cavalry is I
request you might
perhaps want a request
or two. I don't think the
casualties were excessive
in view of the work
which was
done.
